title: Abstract
content: We present GradClip, a method for stabilizing training of deep networks by adaptively clipping gradients. Our approach reduces variance and improves convergence on noisy datasets.

title: Introduction
content: Training deep networks on noisy data often diverges due to exploding gradients. Prior work uses a fixed clipping threshold. We instead adapt the threshold to the running gradient norm.

title: Method
content: At each step we compute the gradient g_t and its norm ||g_t||. We maintain an exponential moving average m_t of the norm. We then rescale the gradient so its norm never exceeds lambda * m_t, where lambda is a hyperparameter. The rescaled gradient is used for the parameter update.

title: Equations
content:
m_t = beta * m_{t-1} + (1 - beta) * ||g_t||
hat_g_t = g_t * min(1, (lambda * m_t) / ||g_t||)
theta_t = theta_{t-1} - eta * hat_g_t

title: Experiments
content: On CIFAR-10-N, GradClip improves final accuracy by 2.1 points over fixed clipping. Training is more stable across five random seeds.

title: Limitations
content: GradClip adds one hyperparameter lambda that must be tuned. On clean datasets the method offers little benefit and can slightly slow convergence.
